name: Terri's Chocolate Mousse Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter, unsalted; cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter an 8-inch springform pan and line it with parchment paper. Dust the paper lightly with flour.
2. To prevent leaks, line the outside of the pan with a double thickness of heavy-duty aluminum foil.
3. Melt chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water, stirring occasionally.
4. Cool slightly.
5. In a large bowl, beat egg yolks with 3/4 cup sugar on high until very light and lemon-colored, about 5 minutes. Scrape down the sides occasionally.
6. Beat in the melted chocolate mixture until well blended.
7. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Gradually add remaining sugar and continue to beat whites until stiff.
8. Gently fold in the remaining egg whites into the chocolate mixture.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
10. Place the springform pan in a larger pan filled with hot water, making sure the water does not reach halfway up the sides of the pan.
11. Bake at 375°F for 15 minutes on the lower third of the oven.
12. Reduce the temperature to 275°F and bake an additional 30 minutes.
13. Turn off the heat, prop the door open slightly, and leave the cake in the oven for 40 minutes.
14. Remove the pan from the water and let the cake cool completely in the pan.
15. Refrigerate overnight until the cake sets.
16. Carefully remove the cake from the pan and invert it onto a serving platter.
17. Allow the cake to come to room temperature before serving.
18. Dust with icing sugar.
19. Serve slices with creme anglaise or whipped cream. SERVES: 8-10
